name: fiftyone-pr-triage
description: Triage FiftyOne GitHub issues by validating status, categorizing resolution, and generating standardized responses. Use when reviewing issues to determine if fixed, won't fix, not reproducible, no longer relevant, or still valid.
capabilities: Categorize issues into five triage states based on investigation
Provide structured workflows for searching codebase and git history
Generate standardized response templates for each triage category
Guide maintainers through systematic decision trees
Document code references with file paths and line numbers
Include commands for GitHub CLI and git operations
author: AdonaiVera
securitySummary: This skill contains only documentation and response templates for GitHub issue triage. All detected patterns are false positives from markdown code examples. No executable code, network calls, file system access, or environment variable reads exist. The skill is pure instructional content.
valueStatement: GitHub issue triage requires consistent categorization and clear communication. This skill provides a structured workflow with decision trees and response templates for FiftyOne repository maintainers.
limitations: Requires GitHub CLI and git to be installed and configured
Designed specifically for FiftyOne repository structure
Does not automatically execute commands or close issues
Cannot reproduce bugs without proper development environment

useCases.title: Maintain healthy issue tracker
useCases.description: Keep issue backlog organized by systematically categorizing and responding to bug reports and feature requests.

useCases.title: Provide consistent user communication
useCases.description: Use standardized response templates to ensure users receive clear explanations for issue resolutions.

useCases.title: Assist with issue management
useCases.description: Help maintainers by following documented workflows to identify duplicates and verify bug reports.

promptTemplates.title: Initial issue triage
promptTemplates.prompt: Triage this FiftyOne issue. Read the full description, search the codebase for related code, check git history for recent fixes, and categorize as already fixed, won't fix, not reproducible, no longer relevant, or still valid.

promptTemplates.title: Bug verification
promptTemplates.scenario: Verify if bug can be reproduced
promptTemplates.prompt: Use fiftyone-pr-triage skill to verify this bug report. Search for related code modules, check recent commits, and determine if the issue is reproducible or already fixed.

promptTemplates.title: Duplicate detection
promptTemplates.prompt: Search closed issues and merged PRs using the fiftyone-pr-triage workflow. Identify if this is a duplicate and generate a response linking to the original issue.

promptTemplates.title: Feature request evaluation
promptTemplates.prompt: Apply the fiftyone-pr-triage decision tree to evaluate this feature request. Determine if it aligns with project scope and generate an appropriate response with clear reasoning.

bestPractices: Always check git history and closed issues before marking an issue as new
Provide specific commit hashes or PR numbers when referencing fixes
Request minimal reproducible examples when issues cannot be verified
antiPatterns: Closing issues without searching for duplicates or related fixes
Using vague explanations that do not reference specific code locations
Skipping the investigation checklist before categorizing issues

faq.question: Does this skill work with repositories other than FiftyOne?
faq.answer: The workflow is designed for FiftyOne but the triage categories and decision trees can be adapted to other projects.

faq.question: Will this skill automatically close GitHub issues?
faq.answer: No. It generates response templates and recommendations but requires human review before posting responses or closing issues.

faq.question: What tools are required to use this skill effectively?
faq.answer: You need GitHub CLI, git, access to the repository, and the ability to run grep and find commands.

faq.question: How does this skill handle security vulnerabilities?
faq.answer: This skill focuses on general issue triage. Security issues should follow separate responsible disclosure procedures.

faq.question: Can I customize the response templates?
faq.answer: Yes. The templates provide a starting structure that you can modify based on specific context and project communication style.

faq.question: What should I do if an issue fits multiple categories?
faq.answer: Follow the decision tree in priority order. For example, if an issue is both fixed and outdated, categorize as already fixed.
